extra_model_fields.expected_outcome: Reviewers can programmatically verify numeric computations and formula provenance; no semantic changes to conclusions.
regression_risks: Small risk of mismatched computed_summary if CSV export or script contains an off-by-one or parsing error; include unit check to compare recomputed values to existing computed_summary.
suggested_iteration_steps: Run verification script to produce computed_summary.json and compare to current actual_outputs.json.
If matches, update expert_memo.md with cell references and attach CSVs and script to the attempt.

metadata_json.priority_reasoning: Addresses minor reproducibility limitation while preserving existing correct calculations and conclusions; moderate effort to add CSVs, formula-export, and a short verification script.

planned_changes: Export every populated workbook sheet to CSV and include the CSV files in the deliverables (e.g., CFD_surrogate_2_sheet1.csv).
Add a 'FORMULAS' sheet or a separate plain-text file that lists each formula used in the workbook (sheet/cell -> formula text).

Update expert_memo.md to include explicit sheet names and exact cell ranges for: source packet values, per-row abs gap and abs pct error computations, support-flag logic, and remediation-cost calculations.

Include a short verification script (Python notebook or script) that reads the provided CSV(s), recomputes abs pct errors and remediation budget checks, and outputs a computed_summary.json identical to actual_outputs.json.
Document in the memo the environment/tooling used to produce the workbook and note any nonstandard construction (e.g., direct OOXML), and confirm whether openpyxl parsing is supported or not.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve reproducibility and formula provenance so reviewers can programmatically verify workbook computations without missing-dependency limitations.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt met all rubric criteria: deliverables present, per-row absolute percent errors and decisions are documented in expert_memo.md and matched the computed_summary in actual_outputs.json. The only verification limitation is that the XLSX workbook could not be programmatically inspected for live formulas (openpyxl unavailable), reducing workbook-level provenance transparency.
